subject: Physics
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 1: MECHANICS
topics: Mechanics is the branch of Physics that deals with the motion of objects and the forces acting upon
them. It is all about understanding how objects move, respond to forces and interact with their
environment. It is divided into kinematics (describing motion) and dynamics (explaining why objects
move). Mechanics 2 comprises of four (4) sub-topics; Work, Energy and Power, Linear Momentum,
activities:  Create a system that applies work, energy, and power concepts.
 Work is the product of the force applied and the distance moved in the direction of the force.
 A joule is the amount of work done when a force of 1N moves an object through a distance of
m in the direction of the force.
 Energy is the ability or capacity to do work.
resources:  Guide learners in designing and testing their systems
 Researching and Brainstorming on the given scenario of mechanical energy (e.g in a simple pendulum,
assessment:  Observe demonstration of problem-solving and critical thinking skills
 Problem-solving: Designing systems for maximising mechanical energy
